back_translation: All you brothers and sisters, when I came to you, I did not come with words that were excellent or with wisdom, while I was proclaiming to you the matter of the testimony concerning God.
Because I had set in my mind that I would not know any thing among you except Jesus Christ, and Him, the one who was (royally) crucified on the cross.
I myself came to you in weakness, in fear, and in much fearful trembling.
Both my message and my proclamation were not by means of words of enticement of wisdom, but by means of the manifestation of the Spirit and of power.
So that the faith of you all would not be founded upon the wisdom of mankind, but be founded upon the power of God.
But among those who have attained maturity, we do speak of wisdom — only not the wisdom of this age, nor the wisdom of the rulers of this age, who are passing away.
But we speak of the (royal) wisdom of God which exists in mystery — that is, the wisdom that has been (royally) hidden, which God has (royally) determined before the ages began, for our glory.
Not one of the rulers of this age has come to know this wisdom; for if they had known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory upon the cross.

But as it is written: "The things which the eye has never seen, and the ear has never heard, and which have never come up into the heart of man — that is, all the things that God has (royally) prepared for those who love Him."
But God has (royally) revealed it to us through the Spirit; for the Spirit (royally) searches all things, even the depths of God.

For what man is there who knows the things which are within a man, except the spirit of that man himself which is within him? In the same way, no one knows the things which are within God, except the Spirit of God only.
But we did not receive the spirit of the world; rather, we received the Spirit which is from God — so that we may know the things that God has (royally) given to us by grace.

And this is what we speak — not by means of words that the wisdom of mankind teaches, but by means of words that the Spirit (royally) teaches: bringing things of the Spirit to be explained to those who are of the Spirit.
But the natural-man does not accept the things that come from the Spirit of God, because they are foolishness to him; and he cannot know them, because these things must be discerned spiritually.
But the spiritual person discerns all things, while he himself does not fall under the discernment-judgment of anyone.
For there is a written word: "Who has known the (royal) thought of the Lord, that he might instruct Him?" But we have the (royal) thought of Christ.
